_comment: Abstract-deadline feed for Research Tracker. The biweekly scheduled task rewrites this file after checking each society. The app fetches it on load and merges any entry whose verifiedOn is newer. 'key' must stay stable so updates match the right meeting. Set unconfirmed=false only when a date is verified from the society's official call for abstracts.

meetings.name: 2027 Annual Meeting
meetings.dates: Mar 3–6, 2027 (paper/poster/ePoster abstracts due 6/8, extended; OVT 7/15; OrthoDome 8/31)

meetings.name: 2027 Annual Meeting
meetings.dates: Podium/E-presentation abstracts due 8/10/2026; ICLs due 7/10/2026; Diplomat Beach Resort, Hollywood, FL

meetings.name: 8th ICRS Summit (Porto)
meetings.dates: Summit Oct 8–10, 2026; abstracts closed (opened 4/1, closed 5/5/2026; posters only, under-40 early career)

meetings.name: 19th World Congress
meetings.dates: May 1–3, 2027, Rotterdam; registration now open; abstract call not yet open (more details to follow)

meetings.name: Annual Meeting
meetings.dates: 2026 abstracts closed (notifications Dec 2025/Aug 2026); Specialty Day Mar 5, 2027; 2027 cycle not yet open

meetings.name: OREF Clinical Research Award
meetings.aim: Supports clinical research that advances patient care across orthopaedic subspecialties.
meetings.criteria: $20,000 award. Application deadline 7/1/2026 (no LOI). See the OREF RFA for eligibility and proposal requirements; apply via proposalCENTRAL.

meetings.name: Mentored Clinician Scientist Grant
meetings.aim: Helps clinician-scientists who already hold a 'K' award build toward independent research funding.
meetings.criteria: $20,000. Applicant must have been awarded an NIH 'K' (career development) award to apply. Application deadline 6/24/2026 (no LOI).

meetings.name: Resident Research Project Grant
meetings.aim: Funds focused research projects led by orthopaedic residents.
meetings.criteria: $5,000. For orthopaedic residents. LOI due 4/22/2026; application deadline 6/24/2026.

meetings.key: OREF|Resident Clinician Scientist Training Research Grant
meetings.name: Resident Clinician Scientist Training Research Grant
meetings.aim: Supports orthopaedic residents pursuing clinician-scientist training through a mentored research project.
meetings.criteria: $20,000. For orthopaedic residents in clinician-scientist training. LOI due 4/22/2026; application deadline 6/24/2026. See OREF RFA on proposalCENTRAL.

meetings.name: ORS/OREF Distinguished Investigator Award
meetings.aim: Honors an investigator for sustained, distinguished contributions to musculoskeletal research.
meetings.criteria: Honorary award (joint with ORS). Nomination-based; application deadline 9/11/2026.

meetings.name: AOSSM-AF Hip Osteoarthritis Grant (Full)
meetings.aim: Funds studies on hip pathology and the rapid development of osteoarthritis that can grow into externally-fundable research programs.
meetings.criteria: In collaboration with the Arthritis Foundation; up to three grants of $250,000 each. Must address an important clinical problem in hip pathology/PTOA and aim to establish a programmatic line of research competitive for NIH/PCORI/DOD funding. LOI due 4/17; full proposals due 6/26/2026.
funded: Sex-specific molecular and mechanical factors in cartilage injury — Paula Hernandez, PhD (2022)
Delivery of chondrogenic genes for articular cartilage repair — Christopher Nagelli, PhD (2021)
Mechanical Loading Effects in OA Progression Following ACL Reconstruction — Camila B. Carballo, PT, PhD (2019)

meetings.name: Sandy Kirkley Clinical Outcomes Grant
meetings.aim: Provides seed or supplemental funding for an outcomes research project or pilot study, honoring Dr. Kirkley's advocacy for rigorous RCTs.
meetings.criteria: $25,000 start-up seed or supplemental funding for an outcome research project or pilot study in orthopaedic sports medicine. Submission deadline 7/31/2026.
funded: Extended Post-Operative Oral TXA after ACLR: A Multicenter RCT — David Bernholt, MD (2025)
Arthroscopic Distal Tibia Allograft vs Latarjet for Subcritical Bone Loss — Ivan Wong, MD (2024)
Evaluating the Efficacy of Erector Spinae Block in Routine Hip Arthroscopy — Benjamin Coobs, MD (2022)

meetings.name: AOSSM-JRF Ortho Allograft Grant
meetings.aim: Advances insight into meniscal or osteochondral allograft transplantation of the knee.
meetings.criteria: In coordination with JRF Ortho; up to $50,000 for a basic science or clinical project on viable osteochondral (OCA) or meniscus allograft transplantation of the knee. Translational/clinical-care advances reviewed favorably. Deadline 8/1/2026.
funded: Understanding OCA structure and matrix nanomechanics to improve integration — Paula Hernandez, PhD (2025)
Biomechanics of segmental meniscus transplant: a human cadaveric study — Alvin Su, MD, PhD (2024)
Nonorthotopic osteochondral allograft transplantation to the femoral head — Christopher Nagelli, PhD (2023)

meetings.name: Arnoczky Young Investigator Grant (Full)
meetings.aim: Supports early-career principal investigators leading an orthopaedic sports medicine research project, with mentorship.
meetings.criteria: Up to $40,000 each for one clinical and one basic science project ($80,000 total). A Young Investigator is an orthopaedic surgeon who has graduated from or is currently in an approved residency program; the PI must perform the primary project role, remain at the parent institution for the project period, and have received less than $25,000 in external funding. Pre-proposals due 5/15; full proposals due 9/20/2026.
funded: Artificial Intelligence for Data and Insights in Young Hip (AIDY) — Yining Lu, MD (2024, Clinical)
Analysis of Noninvasive Biomarkers for ACL Reconstruction — Luke Troyer, MD (2024, Basic)
Sex Differences in FAIS Hip Synovium Leading to Differences in Outcomes — Stephanie Wong, MD (2023, Basic)

meetings.name: Playmaker Grant Lab
meetings.dates: $25,000 each (1–2 grants); LOI due 5/15/2026 (closed); finalists present at the Annual Meeting
meetings.aim: Provides start-up, seed, bridge/gap, or supplemental funding to advance exploratory projects on emerging or novel technology in orthopaedic sports medicine.
meetings.criteria: Sponsored by The Aircast Foundation and Haslam Sports Innovation Center. Three-phase process: 2-page LOI (Phase 1, due 5/15/2026), a ≤15-minute presentation to the AOSSM Research Committee at the Annual Meeting (Phase 2), then a formal AOSSM-format grant application for funded projects (Phase 3). Studies involving human subjects receive priority.
funded: Virtual Reality as a Prevention Tool for Anterior Cruciate Ligament Injury — Estephania Nunez, DAT (2025)
Dislocation- and Surgery-Induced Plasticity of the Glenoid Track — Jonathan Hughes, MD (2025)
Valgus Force on the UCL During Batter's Swing and Hitting Motion — Bradley A. Lezak, MD (2025)

meetings.name: 2026 MAST Research Initiative Grant
meetings.dates: $150,000 total funding pool · new joint AOSSM/AANA/SOMOS grant
meetings.aim: Funds orthopaedic research relevant to the military or general population, jointly sponsored by AOSSM, AANA, and the Society of Military Orthopaedic Surgeons (SOMOS).
meetings.criteria: New for 2026. $150,000 total funding pool. Joint initiative among AOSSM, the Arthroscopy Association of North America (AANA), and the Society of Military Orthopaedic Surgeons (SOMOS) for research pertaining to the military population or general population. Apply via Proposal Central by 8/7/2026.

meetings.name: AANA Research Grant
meetings.aim: Funds clinical or basic science research that advances arthroscopic surgery and disorders amenable to arthroscopic treatment.
meetings.criteria: Funding pool up to $200,000: one project at $100,000 (preference for a multicenter trial), plus $50,000 and $25,000 seed/pilot awards. 2026 submission site is currently closed; future timelines TBA.

meetings.name: OREF/AAOS Clinical Gaps Research Grant
meetings.dates: Administered via OREF — not on current OREF open/upcoming list; cycle dates TBA
meetings.aim: Supports investigator-initiated research addressing clinical gaps in healing, repair, or diagnostics through devices, biologics, or technology.
meetings.criteria: Administered via OREF. Translational, preclinical, or clinical research, preferably including pilot human studies. Cycle dates TBA — confirm on the OREF funding page.
